textByHashes.3075f13aa5be08dd804aa69c62d8df72979dc447: I don't know where to begin.
textByHashes.1f43a7220f9df68749e3fb3b39721214e4ee0894: There was something wrong with him.
textByHashes.a8e0150776bde5778cbd34e7035be3ec6674bd9c: He was always a fan of antiques. One day he bought some old flask from a stall in Lovakengj. He began drinking from the flask wherever he went, and it started to change him.
textByHashes.b3155364369979f45bfa44f8d0f77d7779805719: The flask was cursed. Some dark magic. He stopped being himself. Started saying things. Things he would do to people. To the citizens!
textByHashes.087de4807a83504cc928174f466ad80860aacd60: We did what we had to...
textByHashes.2ea94310c308c71bd3b707634bfe2b30de391b33: No... We never wanted the throne. The throne was always meant for Rose.
textByHashes.8f7511ab5d12ab29adc724dc8c76c5db8764b078: She was never meant to be involved. We were just going to look after things until she was ready. But she found out what we did.
textByHashes.9ea027a0923da083512862fe6fdf3b991d12f0e5: We didn't want to hurt her, but we couldn't risk her telling anyone. This was the only way.
textByHashes.e4e7b4582560b17a1fb1e6b1980afd08ee4ae3ca: I swear to you, we did it for the people.
textByHashes.608526ebb91b191b13bc72fe98d345a6463d736e: We didn't want to kill her. She didn't deserve that. The mage gave us a potion. He said it would stop her revealing the truth.
textByHashes.f9aa6f910cd18a6a7967b6e6e42a3f65401c73e8: There was an Arceuus mage. He was an expert in curses, and he realised what was happening. He gave us the poison for the King.
textByHashes.5c7deaddce82a0dd925d69b8291cb2455c91b516: He never told us his name.
textByHashes.9b069e63043bc687f73ff6fc38405056318e9067: Everything the mage said was true. The King was cursed. We could see it.
textByHashes.d69b904be91144091e2aee2ae8d0e4efb12759a3: Of course it crossed our minds, but we didn't have a choice. The King was deteriorating by the hour.
textByHashes.16c4e576f760b3e2bb714496076fcc766df01a46: Her condition has no cure, but it can be kept in check with regular treatment. He meets with me and gives me the potions she needs.
textByHashes.b4e34d6111fb27b335296b4c7a33bf2987204d44: I've done my research. The potions are safe, and she can't live without them.
textByHashes.f0891af52eca59c8fc9ab163910b92a3687c111d: We meet outside Molch. I think he lives in the temple there, with the lizardmen.
textByHashes.921d021899fcf1fedef23ed8761f1e46acc3344e: Very little. It was David... Sorry, Councillor Andrews, who he contacted first. The rest of us didn't meet him until much later.
textByHashes.0149a1825d2141feef869669b257c4e318ebc5f9: He was an expert in old curses. He could see what was wrong with the King. He told Councillor Andrews and offered his help.
textByHashes.ebe973dbeb19469d669d1ca338cb0ed04be8f5db: By then, it was already too late. The King had deteriorated too much. There was only one solution.
textByHashes.789b1516bab50d4347de71acc1595488caab9b38: You weren't there. You didn't see what was happening to him. There was no time to consider other options. We did what we had to do.
textByHashes.2d3f8024405e0003397ccff24637597b360a9202: That's right. In the temple, I think.
textByHashes.2d2fb619766fe124b8b33ba32dd7c2b16f286720: You're not funny. Do you think we'd have worked with him if we knew all of that? We didn't find out about the lizardman connection until much later. By then, the deed had already been done.
textByHashes.a502ff87df9f3983cc86a2ff70e716a6bd6fe0a7: Sophia wasn't always like that. Before she joined that cult, she was just like the rest of us. The only difference was that she had other beliefs.
textByHashes.6b80ddccdf10c6f2cd43da86303d2d5a0bb09d7d: So what? People should be entitled to their beliefs!
textByHashes.d07c6e509d34caaf88a9c95b604e91a357c79c15: Like I said, she wasn't always like that.
textByHashes.ad3df797bc2d41b2883fd30b61522a62d7381ac2: I know you probably don't believe it, but we always meant for her to take the throne.
textByHashes.4f8697232f190a20a09442b2cf41d91d539dd7fa: She overheard us talking about the King. We couldn't let her tell anyone.
textByHashes.a6401f96d767f724bed40b9a28c42bcbd51027bb: Because without us, who would have been left to rule? We had to do it for the good of Kourend.
textByHashes.9e64661be103e236cea4b5e1b5011911bec3fb1c: She was like a daughter to me!
textByHashes.b3afb3e1aca781b2b450f4a184bdc11d090c3d19: And I have taken care of her every day since! Without me, she wouldn't even be alive.
textByHashes.7fe04e4a0703556e106c8f40e39b56a15c1c0ead: My lords and ladies, there is something I must make you aware of.
textByHashes.af83fd1104882e3ec1e4b7a5e2cfa47ede79389d: It's regarding myself and my fellow councillors. We...
textByHashes.4744a299015df191fce1e8888cc157ca3265e0e5: Don't you lecture me! You have no right! You killed her!
textByHashes.3003d51797d861a8c8a02a80f8af3111e5975ede: They call me a murderer, but they killed Rose! I kept her safe!
textByHashes.508208dfa8dc446fef2e6425cb4e491b14294698: We did it for the good of the kingdom...
textByHashes.d2b55edbf2b85ae1a56c43ccf28dab5f7ee1b9f3: Rose was never meant to get hurt! She overheard us!
textByHashes.57f09f77c283242975d6dac9a8811e063a3ef1d6: Leave me alone! I have nothing to say to you.
